title: From idea → backtest without coding (open-source project)
author: coderchacha
selftext:
https://preview.redd.it/r7r05ke5emtg1.png?width=2547&format=png&auto=webp&s=90bf14956eb239753b9176259bef15f065eb201f

https://preview.redd.it/n7048y26emtg1.png?width=1246&format=png&auto=webp&s=a0484b26b6109770dda493cd4acdd95e08016f7a

Most traders today can *generate* strategies with ChatGPT.

But almost none can actually **run, debug, or properly backtest them**.

That’s the gap I’ve been seeing over and over.

So I started building something to fix it 👇

**Trading Copilot** (open-source):  
[https://github.com/tanmay2893/trading\_copilot](https://github.com/tanmay2893/trading_copilot)

The idea is simple:

* Take any trading idea (even from ChatGPT/Claude)
* Turn it into something you can actually run
* Backtest it without fighting code errors for hours

Under the hood, it uses agentic AI to help with:  
→ generating strategies  
→ running + debugging them  
→ iterating quickly

But the bigger goal is not just a tool.

It’s to **democratize systematic trading**—so you don’t need to be a coder to test and validate ideas.

Right now, most retail traders are stuck between:

* “I have an idea”
* “I saw a strategy”
* “ChatGPT gave me code”

…and:  
❌ it doesn’t run  
❌ results don’t make sense  
❌ no way to iterate

Trying to bridge that gap in an open, community-driven way.

Would love feedback from people here—especially:

* What’s the most painful part of backtesting today?
* What would make you actually *use* something like this?

If you find this interesting, a ⭐ on the repo would really help 🙏  
And if anyone wants to contribute/collaborate, would love to connect.

comments.author: Leading_Internet_625
comments.body: Loved to here this..i will check and tell you for sure

comments.author: Impressive_Air1820
comments.body: This looks awesome. Are you planning to keep it public or launch as a part of a fintech startup?

replies.author: coderchacha
replies.body:
Appreciate it!

Started as a startup idea, but felt too early, so open-sourced it to build in the open.

Still figuring out monetization, but the goal is to make strategy testing accessible to non-coders and grow this as a community effort.

comments.author: unInconnu88
comments.body: I am stuck with back testing properly, so I'll check

comments.author: Expensive-Badger-142
comments.body: This is dope as fuck, good looks

replies.author: coderchacha
replies.body: Thanks man! Would love your feedback on it. Although, I am aware of a few bugs I am currently working on, I would appreciate if you have any feature requests and critical feedback too!

replies.author: Mindless-Stand-9654
replies.body: What kind of bugs are you running into? Also, I think a feature to visualize backtest results would be super helpful for users to understand their strategies better.

replies.author: coderchacha
replies.body:
Thanks for your feedback. I am aware of hallucinations that are relatively frequent if I compare it with tools like Claude Code, etc. Working on fixing those.   
May I ask what do you exactly mean by 'visualize backtest results'? In the charts section, we are already able to view the signals and debug the algorithm if needed. Do you mean something else by 'visualize'?

comments.author: Equal-Ad5322
comments.body:
While daily candles are useful for high-timeframe positioning, I have significant doubts about their validity for intraday strategies. For daytrading, even 1-minute candles lack the precision required to model reality. Backtests should always utilize tick data whenever possible.

As part of my trading platform, I am developing a backtesting research lab focused on extreme performance and efficient memory management. Speed is the ultimate edge in quantitative research. My current engine processes approximately 3M ticks per second per backtest. During parallelized parameter sweeps across 14 threads, I am achieving throughput between 30M and 70M ticks per second, depending on the strategy's mathematical complexity.

It is this level of performance, enabling thousands of parameter permutations to be tested against years of tick data in minutes rather than days, that provides a true competitive advantage. This is especially true when the top-performing candidates are then subjected to rigorous walk-forward optimization.

comments.author: BottleInevitable7278
comments.body: Sorry, but this is almost pure garbage. If you lack the knowledge and you are not more prudent than the Claude Opus 4.6 Extended AI you do not any good edge. It is not that easy as you and many others may think.

replies.author: coderchacha
replies.body:
Fair point - edge matters more than tooling.

But the gap I'm bridging is "has an idea" vs "can actually test it." Claude Code can write a strategy and silently introduce bugs with zero way to catch them. That's exactly what this pipeline tries to handle and it uses Claude Opus and other LLMs under the hood anyway.

This specific workflow doesn't exist yet in an open way, to my knowledge. Curious - is there a version you'd find useful, or do you think the whole premise is flawed?
